title: Reading and Use of English – Part 2
description: For questions 9–16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only ONE word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The ambition to build taller structures has driven architectural innovation (0) for centuries, resulting in the massive skyscrapers that dominate our modern cities. (9) Although early towers were limited by the strength of masonry, the invention of steel frames revolutionized the industry. (10) Such is the height of contemporary buildings like the Burj Khalifa that they require specialized engineering to withstand high-altitude winds. (11) Provided that sustainable materials are used, the next generation of skyscrapers could significantly reduce the carbon footprint of urban areas. (12) Not only do these vertical cities save space, but they also offer a solution to the growing problem of global overpopulation. (13) Regardless of the immense costs involved, developers continue to push the boundaries of what is structurally possible. (14) Not until the introduction of advanced elevator technology did building beyond a few stories become practical for everyday use. (15) Despite the aesthetic appeal of glass and steel, architects are now exploring the use of cross-laminated timber for high-rise construction. (16) Whether or not we will eventually build structures that reach into the upper atmosphere remains a matter of speculation. This ongoing pursuit of height reflects our enduring desire to reach new horizons and redefine our urban environments.

example.text: The ambition to build taller structures has driven architectural innovation (0) for centuries.
example.explanation: Preposition used to indicate a duration of time.

questions.explanation: Conjunction used to introduce a contrast.
Determiner used for emphasis at the start of the clause.
Part of the 'Not only... but also' structure.
Part of the fixed phrase 'Regardless of'.
Part of the 'Not until' structure triggering inversion ('did building... become').
Preposition used to introduce a contrast.
Conjunction introducing a doubt or two options.
